title: astragalus canescens
intro: Astragalus canescens, also known as white milk vetch, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family that is native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and immune-stimulating effects. The health benefits of Astragalus canescens include reducing stress and fatigue, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and flu. Astragalus canescens is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and can also be consumed as a dried root or extract.
